label: MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing

node.label: MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing
node.definition: Sampling, alternating latent-variable estimation, and local factor updates approximate difficult inference problems.
node.why_it_matters: The role of MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing is to connect Bayesian & approximate inference, Graph theory to downstream capabilities including k-means, mixture models & clustering, Hidden Markov models & CRFs, Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs.
examples: substrate for k-means, mixture models & clustering
substrate for Hidden Markov models & CRFs
substrate for Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Bayesian & approximate inference, Graph theory.

dependencies.rationale: MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bayesian & approximate inference.
MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing uses Graph theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.

sentence_units.text: Sampling, alternating latent-variable estimation, and local factor updates approximate difficult inference problems.

The role of MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing is to connect Bayesian & approximate inference, Graph theory to downstream capabilities including k-means, mixture models & clustering, Hidden Markov models & CRFs, Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs.
